content.full_text:
State of California                                                    A-78-402

Fair Political Practices Commission
P O BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO 95801 • FAIR STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

[contact information partially visible]

February 3, 1978

Vincent F. Biondo, Jr.
City Attorney
City of Carlsbad
1200 Elm Avenue
Carlsbad, California 92008

Re: Opinion Request No. 76-078

Dear Mr. Biondo:

     This is in response to your letter to me dated December 7, 1977, seeking a clarification of certain aspects of the advice previously issued by the staff in the referenced matter.

     The letter should be read as requiring that public officials participating in the decisions concerning the Encina Sewage System disclose their financial interests which are within the geographic service area of the System, and not merely the interests found within the boundaries of their participating governmental entity. However, as I indicated to you in the telephone conversation, I think it likely that this disclosure need not be as all-encompassing in terms of subject matter as that required of the city council members of the City of Carlsbad pursuant to Government Code Section 87200, et seq.

     Specifically, in the context of disclosure of financial interests pursuant to Government Code Section 87300, et seq., disclosure could be limited to those types of interests which will foreseeably be materially affected by decisions of the agency in question. Such a limitation would be appropriate in the context of a Code developed for the System. Perhaps limiting disclosure to interests in real property within the service area of the System, and income from investments and income from that type of business entity or source with which the founding bodies of the agency contract on behalf of the System would be appropriate. I have enclosed a model Conflict of Interest Code of a Sewage District which may prove helpful in defining these standards.

Vincent F. Biendp, Jr.
February 3, 1978
Page Two


However, it does not appear that this can be accomplished
within the context of the Code adopted by the City Council of
the City of Carlsbad and the city councilmembers filing pursuant
to Section 87200, et seq. Government Code Section 87309 provides:

No Conflict of Interest Code or amendment shall
be approved by the code reviewing body, or upheld
by a court if it:

(a) Fails to provide reasonable assurance that
all foreseeable potential conflict of interest
situations will be disclosed or prevented;

(b) Fails to provide to each affected person
a clear and specific statement of his duties under
the Code; or

(c) Fails to adequately differentiate between
designated employees with different powers and
responsibilities.

As you may be aware, the Commission has consistently inter-
preted this Section, taken in conjunction with the requirements
of Section 37302, as requiring a Code to specifically enumerate
those types of financial interests to be disclosed. It has
rejected any approach which would leave to the determination
of the designated employee the definition of what interests
may "foreseeably be materially affected" by his decisions.
See Section 87402. See also, Metropolitan Water District v.
Fair Political Practices Commission, 73 Cal. App. 3d 650 (1977).

Accordingly, it is our interpretation that city council-
persons may, at their election, and that of the other agencies
participating in the system, either disclose all interests
reportable under Section 87200 within the geographic
jurisdiction, or merely shift a Code defined reportable
interests for those persons participating in the System in a
more narrow fashion.

In your letter of February 20, 1978, you state that Carlsbad
has only a 1/4 vote in matters relative to the Encina Joint
Sewage System. This diminished voting status previously
made to the Commission which provided that the System had no
governing body. Rather, we were informed that all substantive
decisions were a product of amendment to the joint powers

Vincent F. Biondo, Jr.
February 3, 1978
Page Three


agreement. If, in fact, Carlsbad only exercises one "vote" on a 6 member governing board, we think the System should have its own Conflict of Interest Code.

     Thank you for writing. If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to call on me.

                                            Sincerely,


                                            Kenneth W. Goshorn
                                            Research Specialist III
                                            Conflicts of Interest Division

KWG:mfa

CITY OF CARLSBAD
1200 ELM AVENUE
CARLSBAD, CALIFORNIA 92008
(714) 729-1181

VINCENT F. BIONDO JR.
CITY ATTORNEY

December 7, 1977

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Attn: Mr. Kenneth Goshorn
Conflict of Interests Division

RE: Opinion Request No. 76-078

Dear Mr. Goshorn:

We are in receipt of a letter signed by Michael Bennett,
Executive Director of the Commission, in regards to the
above-referenced opinion request. The letter indicates
that any questions should be directed to you, and we
have some. In particular, we are concerned about the
nature of the disclosure which must be made by the members
of the Carlsbad City Council. The letter indicates that
in making a disclosure they must concern themselves not
only with matters inside the city limits of Carlsbad, but
also anything that occurs within the limits of the service
territory of the Encina Sewage System. That area includes
a large portion of North County. We would appreciate any
clarification you could offer us on the nature of the
disclosure which the Council must make in this larger area.

We would appreciate any assistance you can give us.

Very truly yours,

VINCENT F. BIONDO, JR.
City Attorney

VFB/mla

CITY OF CARLSBAD

1200 ELM AVENUE
CARLSBAD, CALIFORNIA 92008
(714) 729-1181

VINCENT F. BIONDO JR.
CITY ATTORNEY

January 26, 1978


Mr. Michael Bennett
Executive Director
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES
COMMISSION
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

RE: Opinion Request
    No. 76-078

Dear Mr. Bennett:

It is our understanding that the above-referenced opinion
request was dismissed by the Commission and that your
staff responded to the matter by issuing an informal advice
letter. A copy of that letter is enclosed for your ready
reference.

We had occasion to discuss the letter with Mr. Coshorn, of
your staff, in early December. During that conversation we
expressed our concerns with the breadth of coverage for
disclosure which seemed to us to go beyond the requirements
of the Act. The geographical areas served by the Encina
System constitutes a substantial portion of north San Diego
County. We perceive no need to disclose things such as
sources of income located in another city. There is no way
that any decisions we might make in regards to the joint sewer
system could affect existing businesses. The only possible
conflict of interest situation could arise from ownership of
undeveloped real property which could, arguably, be affected
by decisions to expand or not expand the sewer plant. Even
in that case the possible conflict is extremely remote. Our
interest in the plant is limited to a 1/6th vote on most
matters. All an individual councilperson could do would be
one of five votes issuing instructions to our representative
to the joint system in terms of how to exercise Carlsbad's
1/6th vote. It seems to us that 1/5th of a 1/6th vote in
regards to the system is extremely attenuated in terms of
conflict situations.

Mr. Michael Bennett                -2-                January 26, 1978


The purpose of this letter is to request some clarification
of the new reporting requirement which your staff is asserting.  Perhaps after you have reviewed the matter you may
conclude it is not necessary for the members of the City
Council to make the same disclosures in the wider area that
would be required within the city limits. In addition to my
conversation with Mr. Goshorn, we addressed a short letter to
him in this regard on December 7, 1977 but, to date, we have
had no response.  Any assistance you can give us would be
appreciated.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        VINCENT F. BIONDO, JR.
                                        City Attorney

VFB/mla

Enclosure
